summary: Completed backend + frontend E2E testing for Builder page iteration 10. All P0 features verified: Builder page loads reliably, Builder home shows existing team prompts table, Create New flow works with READY template selection and prompt name input, critical bug regression (variable input -> prompt output sync) confirmed fixed for both new and existing drafts, middle pane shows variable key names with separate help text, and /api/prompts/compile supports case-insensitive placeholder substitution.
action_items: No blocking fixes required for requested scope.
Optional: Clean up test drafts with prefix TEST_ITER10_ if needed.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py exceeds 700 lines (1263 lines) - should be modularized by domain routers/services.
Variable sync logic in promptBuilder.js correctly handles case-insensitive matching via resolveVariableValue function.
seed_data_creation: Created test drafts with prefix TEST_ITER10_ during testing (TEST_ITER10_VariableSync, TEST_ITER10_VariableKeyNames). Cleanup test deletes these automatically.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Builder page variable sync bug is confirmed fixed. Key implementation: isPromptManuallyEdited flag resets to false when loading drafts, allowing auto-sync until user manually edits the output textarea. Case-insensitive placeholder matching implemented in both frontend (promptBuilder.js resolveVariableValue) and backend (server.py resolve_variable_value). Variable key names are displayed as labels with help text shown separately below.
rca_of_the_issue: No defects found in requested scope. The variable sync fix works by: (1) Setting isPromptManuallyEdited=false when loading drafts, (2) useEffect syncs compiledOutput to promptEditorText when isPromptManuallyEdited is false, (3) Case-insensitive matching in fillTemplate/resolveVariableValue handles placeholder case differences.
